questions.question: Chickpeas when soaked in water can swell up to three times their volume. The phenomenon involved in this is called _?
questions.explanation: The movement of water through semipermeable membrane from region of higher concentration to region of lower concentration is called Osmosis. The uptake or absorbtion of water by solid substance without forming solution is called Imbibition.

questions.question: Hyponatremia and Hypernatremia are common electrolyte disorders resulting due to imbalance of which of the following?
questions.explanation: Hyponatremia is a low sodium level in the blood. Mild symptoms include a decreased ability to think, headaches, nausea, and poor balance. Hypernatremia is an electrolyte imbalance under which we find high level of sodium in the blood.

questions.question: Which among the following is the correct sequence of the Levels of Organisation found in living organsims?

options.label: A
options.text: Individual – Organ system – Tissue – Cell – Organ

options.label: B
options.text: Organ – Organ system – Tissue – Cell – Individual

options.label: C
options.text: Tissue – Cell – Organ – Organ system – Individual

options.label: D
options.text: Cell – Tissue – Organ – Organ system – Individual.

answer.label: D
answer.text: Cell – Tissue – Organ – Organ system – Individual.

questions.explanation: The correct sequence of the level of Organisation in living organism is :Cell – Tissue – Organ – Organ system – Individual. The cell is basic unit of life and thesmallest level of organisation of the living organisms.In multicellular organisms more than one cell of similar or dissimilar nature combine to form a tissue.Different tissues combone to form distinct organs. A group of organs coordinating to performs a major function forms the organ system.A multicellular individual is formed by the combination of many organ systems working in coordination. Thus Option [D] is correct

questions.question: Niacin is the chemical name of which vitamin?
questions.explanation: The chemical name of Vitamin B3 is Niacin which is a water soluble vitamin. It is an important nutrient. In fact, every part of your body needs it to function properly. As a supplement, niacin may help lower cholesterol, ease arthritis and boost brain function, among other benefits.

questions.question: Night Blindness is caused by deficiency of which of the following Vitamins?
questions.explanation: Night blindness is caused by nearsightedness, cataracts, or vitamin A deficiency. Night blindness (nyctalopia) is the inability to see well at night or in poor light. Its causes include glaucoma medications and cataracts

questions.question: Which among the following acts as a resistance against disease in the body? (UPSC Prelims 1982)
questions.explanation: White blood cells help you fight infection. When one gets sick, white blood cell count is higher than normal. This is because your body is releasing more of these cells to fight the infection.

questions.question: Which of the following represent Dinosaurs? (UPSC Prelims 1989)
questions.explanation: Dinosaurs were warm-blooded reptiles. Mammal bone study sheds light on dinosaur physiology.

questions.question: What term is used for a gene that exhibits multiple effects?
questions.explanation: Pleiotropism describes the genetic effect of a single gene on multiple phenotypic traits. A classic example of pleiotropy is phenylketonuria, a human disease that affects multiple systems but is caused by one gene defect. The underlying mechanism is that the gene codes for a product that is, for example, used by various cells, or has a signaling function on various targets.

questions.question: The study of ear, nose and throat is done in which of the following branches?
questions.explanation: Otorhinolaryngology is a medical specialty that focuses on the ear, nose, and throat.

questions.question: Who among the following discovered Vitamin E?
questions.explanation: Vitamin E was discovered by Evans and Bishop in 1922. Work in the 1930s revealed the chemical structure and biological function of alpha-tocopherol.
